_about: Lookup tables for location normalization. SYSTEMS is the closed enum of canonical Bobiverse star systems (catalog stars w/ real XYZ in stars-near.bin) plus non_physical (no real star). GAZETTEER maps named in-world places to their parent system. MEGASTRUCTURES are large built/topological structures sited at a system but rendered with a non-spatial convention per handoff D1. Confidence reflects source strength.

_confidence_levels.cast: stated in a book Cast-of-Characters appendix
_confidence_levels.timeline: stated in the B1-3 fan timeline
_confidence_levels.wiki: stated in the wiki timeline CSV (timelines/bobiverse-wiki-timeline.csv)
_confidence_levels.cast+timeline: in cast appendix and fan timeline
_confidence_levels.implied: not explicitly stated; inferred from co-located events
_confidence_levels.unresolved: system unknown; needs prose/wiki lookup. DO NOT TRUST.

systems._note: Only local-bubble stars carry real XYZ in stars-near.bin. Non-physical entries either have no real star (Federation Capital is fictional) or are too distant to render in-scene (Sagittarius A* is ~26000 ly — render as directional beacon per handoff D1).

megastructures.name: Heaven's River
megastructures.note: Quinlan megastructure spiral discovered B4-1 C3; sub-locations (Garack's Spine, Three Lagoons, Galen Town, Elbow, Misty Falls, Six Hills, Cedar Rapids, Utopia River, Nirvana River System, Arcadia River System, Transit, Halep's Ending) all render as megastructure-internal nodes.

megastructures.name: Matryoshka Brain
megastructures.note: Hugh's Skippies install at Epsilon Eridani, B4-1 C30. Distinct from Skippyland.

megastructures.name: Hub Zero
megastructures.note: central wormhole network hub, ~1700 ly from contact system, B5 C5/14/52. Off-map distant; non-spatial render.

megastructures.name: Federation Capital
megastructures.note: Pan Galactic Federation capital, B5 C68. Non-spatial.

megastructures.name: Wormhole Network
megastructures.note: network of >300 wormholes B5; render as edge-set, not a node.

off_map_systems.name: Centaurvania
off_map_systems.note: Gunther's home system, destroyed by something. B5 C48. Beyond local bubble.

off_map_systems.name: Roanoke
off_map_systems.note: abandoned alien world reached via wormhole, B5 C52/54/56. Beyond local bubble.

off_map_systems.name: Alien System
off_map_systems.note: wiki's generic label for the first wormhole-network discovery, B5 C5/8/12. Possibly named in prose.

off_map_systems.name: Sagittarius A*
off_map_systems.note: galactic center, ~26000 ly. Render as directional beacon per D1.

vehicles.name: Bellerophon
vehicles.note: the Others' colony-cargo ship recovered B3, used to evacuate Earth. Crewed by Herschel/Neil.

vehicles.name: Fargo
vehicles.note: smaller Bellerophon-class built by Will, B4-2 C13. Ever Onward society.

vehicles.name: Exodus-3
vehicles.note: Sam pilot, to Omicron2 Eri B2C7.

internal_locations.name: Virt
internal_locations.note: default VR space; render as non-spatial overlay

internal_locations.name: BobNet
internal_locations.note: the inter-Bob comms substrate; treat as Virt-equivalent

internal_locations.name: Bobiverse
internal_locations.note: wiki's looser label for in-network scenes

conflicts.desc: Timeline B1C39 says Bender leaves for 'Gamma Leporis A'. Wiki 22-system list has 'Eta Leporis'.
conflicts.resolution: B4-1 C2 (wiki row 219) says Bob 'begins his search for Bender towards Gamma Leporis A, and after several months finds evidence of a possible megastructure at Eta Leporis and heads towards it'. So Bender INTENDED Gamma Leporis A but ended up at Eta Leporis. Both are correct, at different times.

conflicts.desc: Book-2 Cast says Milo found Vulcan/Romulus at 'Epsilon2 Eridani'. Datelines + timeline + wiki say Omicron2 Eridani (=40 Eri).
conflicts.resolution: Cast appendix is wrong; trust dateline/timeline/wiki. Omicron2 Eridani is canonical.

conflicts.desc: Genealogy JSON spells 'Viktor', born 2165. Timeline B2C24 and wiki row 77 spell 'Victor', cloned 2174 alongside 'Pete' (absent from JSON).
conflicts.action: normalize to 'Victor'; add Pete to lineage; reconcile date discrepancy

conflicts.desc: Genealogy 'born' year vs timeline/wiki 'comes online' year disagree for first cohort (2144 vs 2145).
conflicts.action: define edge timestamp semantics: replication_edge.timestamp = activation date, not lineage-creation date. Bob is reanimated 2133 but cohort comes online 2144-2145 after launch.

conflicts.desc: Fan genealogy JSON covers ~through book 2. Book 3+ active Bobs (Marcus, Herschel, Neil, Icarus, Daedalus, Mack, Khan, Loki, Hannibal, Hugh, Lenny, Mud, Conan, Will/Riker, etc.) missing.
conflicts.action: extend from books 3-5 appendices and wiki Description mining

conflicts.desc: B4-2 wiki rows attribute SCUT/autofactory hacking to Starfleet, then revise (row 280: 'Bill no longer believes that Starfleet did the actual hacking'). Final reveal in B5: Skippies (Mud) were involved.
conflicts.action: event 'attribution' field should reflect what was believed at the time; track revisions separately

conflicts.desc: Quin is the Quinlan homeworld; B4-1 C6 implies same system as Heaven's River (Eta Leporis) but does not state it explicitly.
conflicts.action: verify against prose; current gazetteer assumes Eta Leporis with confidence=implied

conflicts.desc: Jabberwocky (Dragons' homeworld, B5) has no system attached in CSV.
conflicts.action: look up in B5 prose; likely off_map_distant

species_overlay._note: Home-system mappings; verify against prose where confidence < high.

Quinlans.note: homeworld Quin; relocated to Heaven's River topopolis. See conflicts.quin-system.
